title: People practicing open defecation (% of population)
definition: People practicing open defecation refers to the percentage of the population defecating in the open, such as in fields, forest, bushes, open bodies of water, on beaches, in other open spaces or disposed of with solid waste.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for open defecation.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Niger, South Sudan, Chad, while the lightest include Albania, United Arab Emirates, Australia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the open defecation pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Open defecation may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Rural open defecation can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Maternal mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Rural basic sanitation can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Open defecation passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.
